calendar.matters:
Petition To Invalidate Purported Trust Amendment; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse; For An Order Enjoining The Trustee From Using Trust Funds
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Off Calendar

Petition To Invalidate Purported Trust Amendment; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse; For An Order Enjoining The Trustee From Using Trust Funds
Off Calendar Mar-20-2017 Continued To Apr-24-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Minton for Stacy Levitt (Petitioner).
Michael Freedman for Joel Levitt.
Robert Cross for Robin Levitt.

Mr. Freedman reported that the Parties agreed on the fundamental issues, that they need to draft the written agreement which can be done one or two weeks, and that they will be filing a petition for approval of the settlement. The Petition to Invalidate Purported Trust Amendment, etc. is continued to April 24, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the settlement agreement.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Purported Trust Amendment; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse; For An Order Enjoining The Trustee From Using Trust Funds
Off Calendar Dec-12-2016 Continued To Mar-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Minton for (petitioner) Stacy Levitt. Michael Freedman for (respondent) Joel Levitt. Robert Cross for Robin Levitt who is present in Court.

Mr. Minton confirmed that the petitioner has received a copy of the trust. The Court stated that the petition needs to be amended. Mr. Minton agreed that an amendment is in order, that they are talking about mediation and would like a 90 day continuance for status. Mr. Cross agreed that it makes sense. Mr. Freedman stated that this case qualifies for Pro Bono Mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 10, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 15, 2017. The Petition to Invalidate Purported Trust Amendment, etc., was continued to and the Parties are to return on March 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Mr. Minton stated that he will file an amended petition if are unable to settle.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Prado. (Not Reported)
